title: Car carrying family of 3 pulled from Seekonk River in Rhode Island
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A car carrying a family of three went into the Seekonk River near Taft Street boat ramp Sunday evening.
First responders including Pawtucket and Rhode Island State Police arrived within minutes to assist in recovery efforts.
Strong currents and poor visibility are challenging the recovery of the vehicle and its occupants, still unidentified.

title: Jamaica Mi Hungry closes restaurant, shifts focus back to food truck
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Jamaica Mi Hungry closes its Downtown Boston restaurant to focus on catering and mobile food truck services.
The food truck will operate in Kendall Square, MIT, and Boston Common on designated days each week.
Chef-owner Ernie Campbell originally started Jamaica Mi Hungry as a catering company in 2012.

title: Report finds former Canton Sgt. Sean Goode used ‘horrific’ language in texts
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Report reveals ex-Canton Sgt. Sean Goode used offensive language towards various ethnic groups and minorities in texts.
Goode resigned and had his police certification suspended amid an investigation into his discriminatory behavior.
Texts also show Goode shared police information, encouraged bribes, and discussed sleeping on the job.

title: Alvin Campbell Jr., serial rapist and AG’s brother, receives life in prison
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Alvin Campbell Jr. sentenced to life for raping five women and attempting a sixth assault between 2017 and 2019.
Campbell, pretending to be an Uber driver, targeted intoxicated women outside Boston bars and clubs, prosecutors described incidents as similar.
Campbell, brother of Attorney General Andrea Campbell, also faces charges for assaulting a correctional officer and two inmates.

title: Officials identify man killed in wrong-way crash in Boston
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Riley Sergi, 28, from Haverhill, was killed in a wrong-way crash on I-93 in Boston.
Antone Carvalho, 81, from Somerset, drove the SUV responsible and faces pending charges.
A family of four was hospitalized after the crash, with no updates on their condition.

title: Germany vs. Paraguay World Cup live coverage: Extra time in Foxborough
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Germany and Paraguay are in extra time at Boston Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup Round of 32.
Germany's Kai Havertz scored the equalizer, tying the match 1-1 after Paraguay initially led with a goal by Julio Enciso.
63,945 fans attended the match, with the winner set to face France or Sweden in Philadelphia's Round of 16.

title: Revolution announce front office shift: Mass. native Chris Tierney named general manager
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Chris Tierney, a Massachusetts native and former Revolution player, is named general manager and chief soccer officer.
Curt Onalfo transitions from sporting director to senior vice president of soccer operations, focusing on player development.
Both Tierney and Onalfo will report to club president Brian Bilello in their new roles.

title: Man allegedly pepper sprays woman jogging in apparent random assault in Maine coastal city
author: John Waller
bullets: A 37-year-old man allegedly pepper sprayed a woman jogging in Rockland, Maine, on Saturday morning.
The suspect was arrested and faces charges including assault and criminal use of disabling chemicals.
Witnesses reportedly chased off the assailant before police apprehended him in the random attack.

title: Noah Kahan asks his audience to please not poop at venues (except in designated areas)
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Noah Kahan requests fans use designated bathroom areas after an incident at a Philadelphia concert.
Kahan addressed fans stealing road signs referenced in his song, urging them to respect Strafford residents.
The musician pledged to cover costs for replacing the repeatedly stolen Alger Brook Road sign.

title: Hiker rescued by helicopter after 50-foot fall in Huntington Ravine
author: John Waller
bullets: A 24-year-old Canadian man fell 50 feet on Mount Washington's Huntington Ravine Trail and was rescued by helicopter.
Rescue crews, including the New Hampshire National Guard, responded after a 911 call and airlifted the hiker to a hospital.
Officials warn the Huntington Ravine Trail is hazardous, requiring ropes and climbing gear, not suitable for casual hikers.
